content: Hi, could you please point me to the original discussion thread? I'd like to understand the details of your thoughts.<:pray:1356053116260454534>

I think the core of the suggestion is to be able to choose to use the normal exalt in place of the OE, even if the OE bar is full, it will still remove 50% of the remaining aliemus, but some units may prefer to have a more frequent use of the basic exalt than using all the aliemus for 1 OE (you can still do this if a unit has 199 aliemus in place of 200, so is mostly for convenience and consistency) .
It depends in the team don't want to give this option for some particular balance or technical reason.
I think.
for exemple, here, we could choose to trigger over exaltation, or simple exaltation, so next turn we would have another simple exaltation left to use.
not even a full exalt, because normally you remove 50% of the remaining aliemus, so you would have only 50 aliemus, making it even less "exploitable"
the most exploitable thing i can think of it the double exalt per turn with winkle E3, but that can already be done if you have a bit less than 200 aliemus, so is really just a consistency boost
double exalt a turn would be to op I believe : /
you can already do it with winkle
it limited to ultra and with winkle aliemus ready though
yeah, that is what i said in the message

For example on Mouchette, it might be better to not OE and use your stun in the first turn, so you need to play carefully in stages before to nto enter the boss stage with full aliemus. If we could choose to not use OE it would be not an issue. I like suggestion!

Like the title suggests - there's a lot of cute/funny emotes in there, but I rarely see someone actually using them in traphase.
I think it would give that part of players who likes to collect things/care about pfp's a bit more "rewards" and some motivation to play traphase, without actually hurting part of player base that don't care about it.

content: The idea is excellent, and we certainly understand that it would be very useful for new players or for creating beginner guides, but there are indeed many difficulties in implementing it.<:dead:1356082627509092372>
✅「Answered」Expiramental Play Testing
Fair, dt it's easy to implement anw
Understandable, thanks for the fast response
Will the Dev Team have any plans to implement this going forward or undetermined?
Because it is indeed useful, it is still under consideration, but I don't want to make empty promises.<:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184>
W Goya, thank you
Thanks
What if there was a rotating boss where you could borrow multiple units? Could somewhat serve the same purpose but would likely be easier to inplement than up or downscaling units
"Crowdfund" four support characters from other players? Haha, maybe.
Intention was to lower your levels so that you can help new players with earlier stages tho
Then borrow low level supports
4 low level units for story..
Speedrun
how can you guarantee the low level unit you’re looking for is even borrowable <:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184>
Ask around
It would promote community interaction <:Ogier_Cheerup:1468259351889318049>

yo do you have every awakener in the game at level 30 talent level 4?

you do? now please never touch your account again we need your supports
four supports is basically the original idea but with several extra hoops implemented it won’t be a fun experience <:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184>
if someone need it i have a lot of units lv 60 with talent 5 or less and just good enough to low dupes

yeah but I’d be willing to bet several people have lv.60 talent 5 units, its different levels of investment that might be more inconvenient to find, and what if different people want different wheel setups

its not a bad idea on paper, but in practice its gonna be a pain in the bum for both the person that wants a support and the person that can give them it but just changed their lineup for the upteenth time

four supports is still just a significantly more convoluted and annoying experience than just mini creative mode which is what the post suggested, it will still spark discussion over showing off clears with this mode if community engagement is your priority since because of no rewards that’s basically all you can do
sure, my point is that it would probably be a lot easier to implement
oh, i would love more options, i just don't know if they will do it

yeah, but if they end up taking the easy route, will it even find use? 

maybe like some big figureheads of the community can ask around to do some clears, but I’d be willing to bet the part of the community that is in online communities is barely a fraction of the whole playerbase and those people probably don’t want to engage with online communities either. For them this feature will just kinda suck, maybe once in a blue moon they might find a close enough of what they want but it might not work out that way

cool but really really annoying feature to maneuver and actually use for discord reddit and maybe facebook but like everyone else will find it borderline unusable
I still think putting in the effort for the original idea will be more worthwhile <:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184>

Yeah, like you had mentioned earlier. It'll definitely feel more exhausting for both the Player borrowing and the one who is having their Character borrowed. Especially when the very same new Players that the old Players wants to teach are the ones that they are borrowing Characters from and not the other way around. It'll also be theoretically more difficult to explain how to beat certain Stages, if it's rotating rather than being able to beat the certain Stage at any time that they want to. Maybe, instead they could have a single Gamemode where they could pick and choose who and how much they'd like to face. For example, the lady & gentleman from the early Chapters of Arc 1 or Myriam (in her Hydra Form with the two Serpents)
